Tests if dividing a Boolean object by undefined results in NaN.

# Purpose
This code is a single assertion statement that tests the behavior of JavaScript's type coercion and arithmetic operations. It checks if the result of dividing a `Boolean` object with the value `true` by `undefined` is `NaN` (Not-a-Number). The `assert` function verifies that the expression `isNaN(new Boolean(true) / undefined) === true` evaluates to `true`, ensuring that the division operation results in `NaN` as expected. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of JavaScript's handling of arithmetic operations involving non-numeric types.
